steps.title: Identify two points on the boundary line
steps.content: The shaded region is bounded by a line. Reading directly from the graph: the line passes through (0, -11) on the y-axis and (-7, -10).

steps.title: Find the slope and equation of the line
steps.content:
Calculate slope using the two points:

$$m = \frac{-10 - (-11)}{-7 - 0} = \frac{1}{-7} = -\frac{1}{7}$$

Using point-slope form with $(0, -11)$:

$$y = -\frac{1}{7}x - 11$$

Multiply both sides by 7 to clear the fraction:

$$7y = -x - 77$$

Rearrange to standard form:

$$x + 7y = -77$$

steps.title: Identify constants and compute the sum
steps.content:
The boundary line is $x + 7y = -77$. The shaded region (above the line) represents $x + 7y \geq -77$.

Comparing to $rx + ty \geq -77$:

$$r = 1 \quad \text{and} \quad t = 7$$

$$r + t = 1 + 7 = 8$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
